channel.title: Comments for page "Icky & Ichabod"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Icky & Ichabod"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A bullet whizzed through one side of Sophie’s skull and out the other. As her body hit the ground, all of her balloons floated away, her chocolate fountain ran dry, and the rain fell unhindered inside of the park.

This line always gives me a jolt. +1

item.title: Author Post - A Wild Clef Appears
item.content:encoded:
So for those who have been following this canon since its inception, you may recall how in Fuller than Full! Icky kills a GOC operative more or less in cold blood, citing the systematic genocide of Freaks by the GOC as justification.

This was extremely controversial among readers, and I'm not making any claims that Icky was objectively justified in killing Jeremy, but with this tale I'm going a little more into the backstory of her justification.

The genocide she's referring to is specifically the Ichabod Campaign mentioned in SCP-4231. This tale is likely the most directly she's ever suffered at the hands of the GOC, witnessing the murder of a child and nearly being killed by Agent Ukulele (Clef). There's also no doubt been numerous lesser impacts on her life due to GOC activity, and this is why she has such an extreme "Us Vs. Them'' ideology. As far as she's concerned, Freaks will never be free until the last Jailor is strangled with the intestines of the last Book Burner.

Thanks to everyone on chat who read this over, and to thefriendlyvandal for writing SCP-4231.
